idea-plugin.name: LLMask
idea-plugin.description:
LLMask — Privacy-preserving code sharing for LLMs

LLMask obfuscates sensitive identifiers and string literals in your C# code before you send it to an AI assistant, then translates the AI's response back to your real names — so your proprietary domain vocabulary never leaves your machine.

Features

  • Whole-file obfuscation — right-click any C# file in the Project view and copy an obfuscated version to the clipboard in one step.
  • Selection obfuscation — context action on any highlighted block for surgical, PSI-aware masking of just the code you need help with.
  • Smart identifier preservation — well-known framework names (BCL, ASP.NET, NUnit, xUnit, Moq, Entity Framework, and more) and single-letter variables are never obfuscated, keeping AI responses meaningful.
  • String literal obfuscation — replaces sensitive string values with neutral placeholders alongside identifier obfuscation.
  • Custom whitelists — add any identifier or string literal to a personal keep-list via a right-click context action or the Settings page, so your chosen names are always left as-is.
  • One-click deobfuscation — paste the AI's response into your clipboard and run LLMask: Deobfuscate clipboard from the Tools menu to restore all original names automatically.
  • Session mapping files — mappings are persisted per solution in .llmask-map.json, with automatic pruning of sessions older than 30 days.

Usage

  1. Right-click a .cs file → LLMask: Obfuscate and copy to clipboard.
  2. Paste into your favourite AI chat.
  3. Copy the AI's response to the clipboard.
  4. Tools → LLMask: Deobfuscate clipboard — done.

Configure the whitelist and toggle string obfuscation under Settings → Tools → LLMask.

action.text: LLMask: Obfuscate and copy to clipboard
